name: mouse-muscle-atlas

src/ontology/run.sh.summary: Shell script for automation or deployment.

src/scripts/run-command.sh.summary: Shell script for automation or deployment.

repo_insight: The 'mouse-muscle-atlas' project's holistic capability is to develop, maintain, and visualize a structured anatomical ontology specifically dedicated to the musculature of mice, operating within the domain of computational biology and biomedical informatics. It aims to provide a standardized, machine-readable, and human-interpretable knowledge base of mouse muscle anatomy, crucial for comparative studies, data integration, and advancing research utilizing mouse models. This repository is intended to be used by researchers and developers to programmatically generate or update this specialized ontology, transform its data, and visualize its hierarchical structure. Users would primarily interact with the 'run.sh' scripts to execute the processing pipeline, which likely converts an OWL ontology into a tree-like representation, subsequently browsable via the 'ontology-tree.html' file for an interactive anatomical exploration. Architecturally, the project highlights an ontology-driven approach using OWL for formal knowledge representation, an automated processing pipeline orchestrated by shell scripts (src/ontology/run.sh, src/scripts/run-command.sh) for reproducibility, and a Python-based transformation (scripts/owl_to_tree.py) to convert the complex OWL data into a more accessible tree format for web-based visualization, thereby democratizing access to complex anatomical data through a user-friendly HTML interface.
